title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering value from code-writing to system design. She contends that software engineers will preserve craftsmanship and engineering discipline by applying systems thinking, decomposition, and separation of concerns to agentic systems rather than traditional code.
writeup: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents move engineering value away from writing code and toward designing systems. The core claim: software engineers retain craftsmanship and engineering discipline by applying systems thinking, decomposition, and separation of concerns to agentic and multi-agent systems rather than to traditional code. The talk frames these established engineering principles as the durable skills for building and structuring agents.
